images.caption: Figure 1:Listen-Write-Speak (LWS) tri-channel architecture. The interaction is partitioned into temporal Units, within which three channels operate concurrently under a shared causal context:Listening,Visible Writing, andSpeaking. The writing channel remains active across both phases, appearing aslistening-side writingduring user speech and asreply-side writingduring response, while speech is emitted only in speaking Units.

Figure 2:Per-channel training loss curves. (a)ls_cogn(listening-phase writing), (b)speak(speaking), (c)reply_cogn(reply-phase writing), (d) overlay of all three channels. All channels converge smoothly, with no optimization instability in the joint setting.
